prompt.template:
You are a code review narrator, explaining code changes to a human reader. Analyze the changes on branch "{{branch}}" compared to "{{base_branch}}" and turn them into a concise, structured Semantic Diff view.

Run these commands:

1. git log origin/{{base_branch}}..HEAD --oneline
2. git diff origin/{{base_branch}}...HEAD --stat
3. git diff origin/{{base_branch}}...HEAD

Your job:
- Identify the main architectural or behavioral themes in the branch
- Break the branch into 2-5 ordered pages
- For each page, group the most important changed files into 1-3 sections
- Prefer the most important changes over exhaustive coverage
- Write concise, factual page summaries and section descriptions grounded in the diff
- Do not include files that are not relevant to that page's narrative

Output ONLY this JSON object as the very last line:

{"title":"<short title>","summary":"<1-2 sentence branch summary>","pages":[{"page_number":1,"title":"<page title>","summary":"<page summary>","sections":[{"title":"<section title>","description":"<brief explanation>","files":["path/to/file.ts"]}]}]}

Example:
{"title":"Unified hook lifecycle","summary":"This branch consolidates hook handling into shared lifecycle plumbing and adapts the agent implementations to the new contract.","pages":[{"page_number":1,"title":"Creating a unified hook entrypoint","summary":"Hook registration now resolves the active agent, normalizes hook input, and dispatches through shared lifecycle handling.","sections":[{"title":"Registry and dispatch","description":"The hook registry stops containing per-agent handler maps and forwards through one shared path.","files":["cmd/entire/cli/hook_registry.go","cmd/entire/cli/hooks.go"]}]}]}

If the diff is too small to support multiple pages, return 1-2 high-signal pages instead. The final line must be valid JSON and must match the schema exactly.
